hawkish_phrases: inflation would remain undesirably elevated for still some time
core inflation was projected to remain above 2% over the entire horizon
risks to inflation projections were primarily on the upside
a larger increment would be appropriate at the September meeting if the medium-term outlook were to persist or deteriorate
increasing risks of higher inflation becoming more entrenched and persistent
dovish_phrases: the war continued to be a significant downside risk to growth
supply shocks suggested a lengthening, rather than a shortening of the relevant policy horizon
no second-round effects or wage-price spirals could be observed
most measures of longer-term inflation expectations appeared to be still broadly anchored
medium-term orientation of the ECB's monetary policy strategy and with the prevailing uncertainty on the growth outlook
summary: The Governing Council unanimously decided to end net asset purchases as of 1 July and announced its intention to raise rates by 25 basis points in July, signaling a larger increment would be appropriate in September if the medium-term inflation outlook persists or deteriorates, with inflation projected at 6.8% in 2022, 3.5% in 2023, and 2.1% in 2024, with risks tilted to the upside.
